title: rhexia angustifolia
intro: Rhexia angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved marsh pink, is a perennial wildflower native to the southeastern United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects, which are due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rhexia angustifolia include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to soothe digestive problems and reduce fever. Rhexia angustifolia can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine to make salves and creams for topical application.
